objective: Create internal/git/diff.go — (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error) — reproducing commit-pi's md-vs-other staged-diff capture: markdown files captured PER-FILE via `git diff --cached -- <file>` capped at MaxMdLines lines (head -n semantics), everything-else captured in ONE `git diff --cached -- <contract pathspec exclusions>` invocation capped at MaxDiffBytes TOTAL bytes (head -c), concatenated markdown-first; returns ("", nil) — NOT an error — when nothing staged. Defines DiffSettings{MaxMdLines, MaxDiffBytes} in package git (M5 config not yet built). Plus internal/git/diff_test.go (white-box package git, stdlib testing, REAL git binary via the S2 harness) covering the 5 contract MOCKING scenarios.
context:
# PRP — P1.M3.T3.S1: internal/git/diff.go — StagedDiff(cfg) (string, error)

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Add the staged-diff capture primitive to `package git`: `(g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)` that faithfully reproduces the proven `commit-pi` md-vs-other capture — markdown files captured PER-FILE with a per-file line cap (head -n semantics), everything-else captured in ONE `git diff --cached -- <exclusions>` invocation capped at a TOTAL byte budget (head -c semantics), the two concatenated markdown-first. This is the diff payload that `prompt.AssemblePayload` (P1.M4.T1.S3) and the generate orchestrator (P1.M6.T1.S1) feed to the coding agent.

**Deliverable**: TWO NEW files in the EXISTING `internal/git` package:
- `internal/git/diff.go` — defines `type DiffSettings struct { MaxMdLines int; MaxDiffBytes int }` (the small cfg; M5 `config.Config` does NOT exist yet — see Why) and `func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)` plus two unexported helpers `capLines(s string, n int) string` (head -n) and `capBytes(s string, n int) string` (head -c). Uses the shipped, unexported `(g *Git) run(args ...string)` exec seam against the REAL git binary (no go-git, no sh -c). Plain `package git` line (git.go OWNS the `// Package git` doc).
- `internal/git/diff_test.go` — white-box `package git`, stdlib `testing` ONLY, drives the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0) via the shipped S2 harness (`newTempRepo`/`writeFileStage` in gittestutil_test.go), one behavior per `Test*`. Covers the 5 contract MOCKING scenarios verbatim.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./internal/git/`, `go vet ./internal/git/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/git/` empty; `go test ./internal/git/` PASS (5 diff scenarios + existing S1/S2/T2 tests unaffected); `go test ./...` whole-module green. StagedDiff returns `("", nil)` — NOT an error — when nothing is staged; markdown diff is per-file head -n capped and placed BEFORE the head -c capped other diff; `.lock/.snap/.map/vendor/*` excluded from other_diff. stdlib-only imports; no go.mod/go.sum change; the shipped git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing*.go, main.go, Makefile, internal/ui, internal/provider untouched; no `go mod tidy`; `// Package git` doc appears exactly once (in git.go).

## Why

- **The diff payload is the agent's primary input.** `prompt.AssemblePayload` (M4.T1.S3) and the generate two-nested-loop orchestrator (M6.T1.S1) feed `StagedDiff`'s output to the coding agent. Getting the capture mechanics, caps, and exclusions byte-faithful to the proven `commit-pi` reference (reference_impl.md §1) is what makes the generated message reflect the actual staged changes.
- **md-vs-other split exists to apply DIFFERENT caps.** Markdown/docs can be huge; the reference gives each `.md`/`.markdown` file a PER-FILE 100-line cap (head -n) so one giant doc can't drown the context, while non-md code gets a single TOTAL 300KB byte cap (head -c) across the whole other-diff command. StagedDiff is exactly this split, ported to Go.
- **The M5 config package does NOT exist yet — `cfg` MUST live in `package git`.** M3 precedes M5 in the DAG (plan_overview.md). decisions.md §2 lists `StagedDiff(cfg)`; plan_overview.md §2.2 establishes that pre-config layers take "scalar settings / a small prompt.Settings" rather than importing config. So define `DiffSettings` HERE, with field names ALIGNED to the future M5 `Config` fields (`MaxMdLines`, `MaxDiffBytes`) so M6 bridges `config.Config → git.DiffSettings` field-for-field with no rename. This keeps M3 self-contained and testable now.
- **Faithful port, no go-git, no shell (PRD §22.3/§19).** Every git call is a `[]string` run via `exec.Command` (the shipped `run` seam); pathspec tokens like `:!*.lock` are passed as literal args, never interpolated into a shell string — shell injection is structurally impossible. This matches the proven reference semantics exactly (external_deps.md §D).

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/git/diff.go`, FIRST line a PLAIN `package git` (git.go OWNS the `// Package git` doc — do NOT duplicate it; mirror how plumbing.go/gittestutil_test.go use a plain package line + a leading file-level comment explaining what THIS file adds). Imports: stdlib ONLY — `strings` (and `errors` ONLY if you explicitly `errors.As` the diff errors; see Task notes — it is optional since StagedDiff surfaces run errors as-is). NO go-git, NO testify, NO new dep (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).
2. **`DiffSettings` struct** — `type DiffSettings struct { MaxMdLines int; MaxDiffBytes int }`, BOTH fields exported, with a godoc citing FR1-FR5 / decisions.md §6 defaults. It is the small cfg the contract names. Field names mirror the future M5 `Config` fields.
3. **`StagedDiff`** — `func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)`:
   - **Zero-value clamp (defensive):** `if cfg.MaxMdLines <= 0 { cfg.MaxMdLines = 100 }`; `if cfg.MaxDiffBytes <= 0 { cfg.MaxDiffBytes = 300000 }`. cfg is passed BY VALUE so this mutates only the local copy. This keeps M3 testable without M5 and prevents a zero-value footgun (a 0 cap would truncate everything).
   - **md_files:** `mdOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", "*.md", "*.markdown")`; on err `return "", err` (surface the typed `*ExitError` as-is). Parse `mdOut` by splitting on `"\n"`, appending each non-empty line (defensively `strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")` only — do NOT `TrimSpace` a filename, it would corrupt names with leading/trailing spaces; preserve them).
   - **markdown_diff (PER-FILE line cap):** for each file in md_files: `d, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--", file)`; on err `return "", err`; append `capLines(d, cfg.MaxMdLines)` to a `strings.Builder`. (Each file's diff is independently head -n capped, THEN concatenated — NOT a total line cap.)
   - **other_diff (TOTAL byte cap):** `otherOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--", ":!*.lock", ":!package-lock.json", ":!pnpm-lock.yaml", ":!yarn.lock", ":!*.snap", ":!*.map", ":!vendor/*", ":!.md", ":!.markdown")` — pass EXACTLY these tokens verbatim (the `:!.md`/`:!.markdown` are intentionally the literal no-star form — see the CRITICAL gotcha below; do NOT change to `:!*.md`); on err `return "", err`. Then `otherDiff := capBytes(otherOut, cfg.MaxDiffBytes)`.
   - **Concatenate:** `result := markdownBuilder.String() + otherDiff` (plain concatenation, NO separator — the per-file and other diffs already end in `\n` from git, matching reference `diff = markdown_diff + other_diff`).
   - **Empty contract:** `if result == "" { return "", nil }` — NOT an error when nothing is staged (the CLI/generate layer decides auto-stage/exit per FR5). Else `return result, nil`.
   - godoc on StagedDiff cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, decisions.md §9, FR1-FR5, and the ("", nil) empty contract.
4. **`capLines(s string, n int) string`** — head -n semantics: if `n <= 0` return `""`; iterate BYTES counting `'\n'`; return `s[:i+1]` immediately after the Nth newline (inclusive of that newline); if fewer than N newlines, return the whole `s` (matches `head -n N`, including a final partial line). godoc: "head -n N equivalent".
5. **`capBytes(s string, n int) string`** — head -c semantics: if `n <= 0` return `""`; `if len(s) > n { return s[:n] }`; else return `s` (cuts at a byte boundary, may split a UTF-8 rune — acceptable, matches `head -c`). godoc: "head -c N equivalent".
6. NEW file `internal/git/diff_test.go`, `package git` (WHITE-BOX — composes the shipped S2 harness `newTempRepo`/`writeFileStage`; mirror plumbing_test.go's `package git` posture), stdlib `testing` ONLY (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum). Cases (one behavior per `Test*`; see Implementation Tasks for exact code):
   - `TestStagedDiff_MarkdownCappedPerFile` — per-file md line cap (head -n), using a SMALL MaxMdLines for determinism.
   - `TestStagedDiff_OtherCappedTotalBytes` — total other byte cap (head -c), using a SMALL MaxDiffBytes for determinism.
   - `TestStagedDiff_ExcludesLockSnapMapVendor` — `.lock/.snap/.map/vendor/*/package-lock.json` excluded from other_diff.
   - `TestStagedDiff_MdBeforeOther` — both md+other present, concatenated md-FIRST.
   - `TestStagedDiff_NothingStaged` — nothing staged → `("", nil)` (NOT an error).
7. **Scope / boundary**: ONLY create `internal/git/diff.go` and `internal/git/diff_test.go`. Do NOT modify the shipped git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing.go/plumbing_test.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, internal/provider. Do NOT run `go mod tidy`. Do NOT create the M5 config package (out of scope — M5 is a later milestone). The `// Package git` doc comment must appear EXACTLY ONCE (in git.go); diff.go uses a plain `package git` line.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `internal/git/diff.go` exists with a PLAIN `package git` line (no duplicate package doc), imports ONLY stdlib (`strings`, optionally `errors`), and defines exported `DiffSettings{MaxMdLines int; MaxDiffBytes int}` + `func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)` + unexported `capLines`/`capBytes`.
- [ ] StagedDiff clamps non-positive cfg fields to the contract defaults (MaxMdLines→100, MaxDiffBytes→300000) on its BY-VALUE copy; captures md_files via `git diff --cached --name-only -- '*.md' '*.markdown'`; per-file `git diff --cached -- <file>` head -n capped (PER-FILE) and concatenated; `git diff --cached --` with the EXACT contract exclusion pathspec tokens, head -c capped (TOTAL); concatenates markdown_diff + other_diff; returns `("", nil)` when the result is empty.
- [ ] The exclusion pathspec uses the EXACT contract tokens verbatim, including the literal no-star `:!.md` `:!.markdown` (faithful to the reference — see the CRITICAL gotcha).
- [ ] `internal/git/diff_test.go` is white-box `package git`, stdlib `testing` ONLY, composes the shipped S2 harness, drives the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0), and PASSES all 5 MOCKING scenarios: md per-file line cap; other total byte cap; `.lock/.snap/.map/vendor/*/package-lock.json` excluded; md+other present md-first; nothing staged → `("", nil)`.
- [ ] `go build ./internal/git/` and `go vet ./internal/git/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/git/` empty; `go test ./internal/git/` PASS; `go test ./...` whole-module green (Makefile `test` target green; S1/S2/T2 + ui + provider unaffected).
- [ ] Mode A docs: godoc on `DiffSettings` and `StagedDiff` cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, decisions.md §9, FR1-FR5. main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui/internal/provider and the shipped git*.go/plumbing*.go/gittestutil_test.go unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML; no `go mod tidy`; `// Package git` appears exactly once.

### Known Gotchas of our codebase & Library Quirks
```go
// ★★★ CRITICAL PATHSPEC GOTCHA ★★★ — the contract's other_diff pathspec uses the
// LITERAL no-star tokens ':!.md' and ':!.markdown'. In git 2.54 a pathspec WITHOUT a
// wildcard is a LITERAL/PREFIX match: ':!.md' excludes ONLY a file named exactly ".md"
// (or a ".md/" subtree), NOT "README.md"/"doc.md". Verified: with this pathspec, .md
// files STILL appear in other_diff. This is FAITHFUL to the proven commit-pi reference
// (reference_impl.md §1 uses ':!.md' verbatim) — do NOT "fix" it to ':!*.md' (that would
// diverge from the pinned contract/reference). Consequence: markdown content appears in
// BOTH the per-file markdown_diff AND other_diff. Therefore: DO NOT write a test asserting
// "md excluded from other_diff" — it would FAIL. The '.lock/.snap/.map/vendor/*' exclusions
// DO work (they use the '*'/dir glob forms); test those instead.

// CRITICAL (run returns RAW stdout, trailing "\n" included): apply capLines/capBytes to the
// RAW run output WITHOUT trimming first — head -n/head -c operate on the raw stream, so
// trimming before capping would shift the byte/line boundary. (RevParseHEAD/WriteTree trimmed
// because they wanted a SHA; StagedDiff must NOT.)

// CRITICAL (per-FILE vs TOTAL cap scope): the md line cap is applied to EACH per-file
// 'git diff --cached -- <file>' output INDEPENDENTLY, then concatenated — it is NOT a total
// line budget across all md files. The byte cap is applied ONCE to the whole single other_diff
// command output (TOTAL). Do not mix these up.

// GOTCHA (zero-value footgun): a DiffSettings{0,0} would cap md to 0 lines and other to 0 bytes
// (everything truncated). StagedDiff CLAMPS non-positive fields to the contract defaults
// (MaxMdLines->100, MaxDiffBytes->300000) on its BY-VALUE copy — defensive, keeps M3 testable
// without M5's Config.Default().

// GOTCHA (md_files parsing): 'git diff --cached --name-only' prints one path per line with a
// trailing "\n". Split on "\n" and skip empty elements (the trailing empty from the final newline).
// Only TrimRight "\r" defensively (CRLF); do NOT strings.TrimSpace a FILENAME — it would corrupt
// names with intentional leading/trailing spaces. Filenames with embedded newlines are a
// pathological edge the reference also ignores (line-based parsing).

// GOTCHA (no separator on concat): concatenate markdown_diff + other_diff with NO added separator.
// Each per-file diff and the other diff already end in "\n" from git, so they are naturally
// newline-separated. Adding "\n\n" would diverge from the reference's 'diff = markdown_diff + other_diff'.

// GOTCHA (white-box by necessity): run() is UNEXPORTED -> diff.go AND diff_test.go MUST be
// 'package git' (white-box), exactly like plumbing.go/plumbing_test.go. A 'package git_test'
// (black-box) file CANNOT call g.run NOR the unexported capLines/capBytes NOR the S2 helpers.

// GOTCHA (go vet, not go build, compiles _test.go): 'go build ./internal/git/' compiles ONLY
// non-test files; 'go vet ./internal/git/' compiles/vets _test.go too. Run BOTH gates.

// GOTCHA (no go mod tidy): diff.go is stdlib-only; tidy is irrelevant and risky (P1.M1.T1.S1
// precedent — it can strip go-toml which has no importer until M5).
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data models / structure
```go
// internal/git/diff.go
package git

import "strings"

// DiffSettings configures StagedDiff's caps. Fields mirror the future M5
// config.Config fields so the generate layer bridges them field-for-field.
type DiffSettings struct {
	MaxMdLines   int // per-FILE line cap for each .md/.markdown diff (head -n); default 100.
	MaxDiffBytes int // TOTAL byte cap for the other-diff command output (head -c); default 300000.
}

// capLines is the head -n N equivalent: everything up to and including the Nth newline.
func capLines(s string, n int) string { /* ... */ }

// capBytes is the head -c N equivalent: the first N bytes.
func capBytes(s string, n int) string { /* ... */ }

func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error) { /* ... */ }
```

### Implementation Tasks (ordered by dependencies)
```yaml
Task 1: CREATE internal/git/diff.go
  - FIRST LINE `package git` (PLAIN — git.go OWNS the '// Package git' doc; do NOT duplicate it). Add a leading file-level comment explaining THIS file adds the staged-diff capture (StagedDiff) over the shipped run seam (mirror plumbing.go's file header).
  - IMPORTS: stdlib ONLY — "strings" (and "errors" ONLY if you wrap/errors.As — optional since StagedDiff surfaces run errors as-is). NO go-git/testify/new dep (do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum).
  - DEFINE `type DiffSettings struct { MaxMdLines int; MaxDiffBytes int }` (both exported) with godoc citing FR1-FR5 + decisions.md §6 defaults.
  - IMPLEMENT `func capLines(s string, n int) string`: `if n <= 0 { return "" }`; byte loop `for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ { if s[i] == '\n' { lines++; if lines == n { return s[:i+1] } } }`; `return s` (fewer than n newlines).
  - IMPLEMENT `func capBytes(s string, n int) string`: `if n <= 0 { return "" }; if len(s) > n { return s[:n] }; return s`.
  - IMPLEMENT `func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)`:
      * clamp: `if cfg.MaxMdLines <= 0 { cfg.MaxMdLines = 100 }; if cfg.MaxDiffBytes <= 0 { cfg.MaxDiffBytes = 300000 }`.
      * mdOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", "*.md", "*.markdown"); if err != nil { return "", err }.
      * parse mdFiles: split mdOut on "\n", skip empty, TrimRight(line,"\r") only.
      * var md strings.Builder; for _, f := range mdFiles { d, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--", f); if err != nil { return "", err }; md.WriteString(capLines(d, cfg.MaxMdLines)) }.
      * otherOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--", ":!*.lock", ":!package-lock.json", ":!pnpm-lock.yaml", ":!yarn.lock", ":!*.snap", ":!*.map", ":!vendor/*", ":!.md", ":!.markdown"); if err != nil { return "", err }.
      * result := md.String() + capBytes(otherOut, cfg.MaxDiffBytes).
      * if result == "" { return "", nil }; return result, nil.
      * godoc cites PRD §22.3/§19, reference_impl.md §1, external_deps.md §D, decisions.md §9, FR1-FR5, the per-file/TOTAL cap scope, and the ("", nil) empty contract.

Task 2: CREATE internal/git/diff_test.go
  - `package git` (WHITE-BOX — composes the S2 harness newTempRepo/writeFileStage; mirror plumbing_test.go). stdlib `testing` ONLY (import "strings", "testing"; "fmt" if needed for content generation). NO go.mod change.
  - TestStagedDiff_MarkdownCappedPerFile: g:=newTempRepo(t); write a .md with >MaxMdLines changed lines (e.g. 60 lines via a loop / strings.Repeat); writeFileStage; cfg:=DiffSettings{MaxMdLines:5, MaxDiffBytes:300000}; got,err:=g.StagedDiff(cfg); err==nil; rawMd,_:=g.run("diff","--cached","--","<file>"); assert len(strings.Split(rawMd,"\n"))>5 (cap matters) AND strings.HasPrefix(got, capLines(rawMd,5)) (the per-file md diff is head -n capped to 5 and placed FIRST).
  - TestStagedDiff_OtherCappedTotalBytes: g:=newTempRepo(t); write a large NON-md file (e.g. strings.Repeat("x\n", 2000)); writeFileStage; cfg:=DiffSettings{MaxMdLines:100, MaxDiffBytes:64}; got,err:=g.StagedDiff(cfg); err==nil; assert len(got) <= 64 AND got == capBytes(<rawOther>,64) (compute rawOther via g.run("diff","--cached","--",":!*.lock",...same tokens...) or just assert len(got)<=64 and len(got)>0). md_files empty so result == other_diff only.
  - TestStagedDiff_ExcludesLockSnapMapVendor: g:=newTempRepo(t); writeFileStage a.lock,b.snap,c.map,vendor/v.go,package-lock.json,AND main.go; cfg large caps; got,err:=g.StagedDiff(cfg); err==nil; assert strings.Contains(got, "+keep") / main.go marker AND !strings.Contains(got,"lock-content") && !snap && !map && !vendor && !package-lock (use distinctive per-file content markers). Proves exclusions.
  - TestStagedDiff_MdBeforeOther: g:=newTempRepo(t); writeFileStage a SMALL doc.md AND main.go (distinctive content markers); cfg:=DiffSettings{MaxMdLines:100, MaxDiffBytes:300000}; mdDiff,_:=g.run("diff","--cached","--","doc.md"); got,err:=g.StagedDiff(cfg); err==nil; assert strings.HasPrefix(got, mdDiff) (md-FIRST) AND strings.Contains(got, goContentMarker) (other present).
  - TestStagedDiff_NothingStaged: g:=newTempRepo(t) (unborn, nothing staged); got,err:=g.StagedDiff(DiffSettings{}); assert err==nil AND got=="" (the ("",nil) non-error contract; DiffSettings{} exercises the zero-value clamp to defaults too).
  - NAMING: TestStagedDiff_<Scenario>; one behavior per function; use distinctive content strings (e.g. "MDUNIQUE123\n") so Contains assertions are unambiguous.
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details
```go
// head -n N equivalent (byte loop, matches `cmd | head -n N` exactly incl. a final partial line).
func capLines(s string, n int) string {
	if n <= 0 {
		return ""
	}
	lines := 0
	for i := 0; i < len(s); i++ {
		if s[i] == '\n' {
			lines++
			if lines == n {
				return s[:i+1] // include up to & including the Nth newline
			}
		}
	}
	return s // fewer than n newlines -> whole string
}

// head -c N equivalent (cuts at a byte boundary; matches `cmd | head -c N`).
func capBytes(s string, n int) string {
	if n <= 0 {
		return ""
	}
	if len(s) > n {
		return s[:n]
	}
	return s
}

// The other_diff pathspec EXACTLY as pinned by the contract (do NOT change :!.md -> :!*.md; see gotcha).
func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error) {
	if cfg.MaxMdLines <= 0 {
		cfg.MaxMdLines = 100
	}
	if cfg.MaxDiffBytes <= 0 {
		cfg.MaxDiffBytes = 300000
	}
	mdOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", "*.md", "*.markdown")
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	var md strings.Builder
	for _, line := range strings.Split(mdOut, "\n") {
		line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
		if line == "" {
			continue
		}
		d, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--", line)
		if err != nil {
			return "", err
		}
		md.WriteString(capLines(d, cfg.MaxMdLines))
	}
	otherOut, err := g.run("diff", "--cached", "--",
		":!*.lock", ":!package-lock.json", ":!pnpm-lock.yaml", ":!yarn.lock",
		":!*.snap", ":!*.map", ":!vendor/*", ":!.md", ":!.markdown")
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	result := md.String() + capBytes(otherOut, cfg.MaxDiffBytes)
	if result == "" {
		return "", nil // nothing staged is NOT an error (FR5; CLI/generate decides auto-stage/exit)
	}
	return result, nil
}
```

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `DiffSettings` and `StagedDiff` cites PRD §22.3 (shell out to real git, no go-git), §19 (no sh -c), reference_impl.md §1 (the exact md-vs-other capture pipeline), external_deps.md §D (verified git 2.54 pathspec), decisions.md §9 (porting map), and FR1-FR5 (caps + exclusions + the ("", nil) empty contract). This is the ONLY documentation this task produces (inline godoc). Do NOT create README.md, docs/, or providers/*.toml here. This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT change the contract pathspec `:!.md`/`:!.markdown` to `:!*.md` — the literal no-star form is FAITHFUL to the proven reference; `:!.md` does not exclude `README.md` by design (git literal pathspec). Do NOT write a test asserting "md excluded from other_diff".
- ❌ Do NOT trim `run`'s raw stdout before capping — head -n/head -c operate on the raw stream; trimming shifts the boundary.
- ❌ Do NOT apply the md line cap as a TOTAL across all md files — it is PER-FILE (each `git diff --cached -- <file>` capped independently, then concatenated). The byte cap is the TOTAL one (on the single other command).
- ❌ Do NOT add a separator (`\n\n`) between markdown_diff and other_diff — plain concatenation matches the reference.
- ❌ Do NOT import go-git/testify/any dep — stdlib only (PRD §22.3); do NOT run `go mod tidy`.
- ❌ Do NOT make diff.go/diff_test.go `package git_test` — they MUST be white-box `package git` to call the unexported `run`/`capLines`/`capBytes` and the S2 helpers.
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate the `// Package git` doc — it is owned by git.go; use a plain `package git` line.
- ❌ Do NOT return an error when nothing is staged — return `("", nil)` (FR5; the CLI/generate layer decides auto-stage/exit).
- ❌ Do NOT create the M5 config package or import it — DiffSettings lives HERE (M5 is a later milestone).
- ❌ Do NOT touch git.go/git_test.go/gittestutil_test.go/plumbing*.go, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui, internal/provider.

successCriteria.description: internal/git/diff.go exists with a PLAIN `package git` line (no duplicate // Package git doc), imports ONLY stdlib (`strings`, optionally `errors`), and defines exported `DiffSettings{MaxMdLines int; MaxDiffBytes int}` + `func (g *Git) StagedDiff(cfg DiffSettings) (string, error)` + unexported `capLines(s string, n int) string` + `capBytes(s string, n int) string`.

capLines reproduces head -n N (returns everything up to and including the Nth newline; whole string if fewer than N newlines; empty if n<=0) and capBytes reproduces head -c N (first n bytes; whole string if shorter; empty if n<=0) — both operating on the RAW run() output with NO pre-trim.

StagedDiff clamps non-positive cfg fields to the contract defaults (MaxMdLines->100, MaxDiffBytes->300000) on its BY-VALUE copy; captures md_files via `git diff --cached --name-only -- '*.md' '*.markdown'`; for each md file runs `git diff --cached -- <file>` and applies capLines PER-FILE then concatenates; runs `git diff --cached --` with the EXACT contract exclusion tokens (including the literal no-star `:!.md` `:!.markdown`) and applies capBytes TOTAL; concatenates markdown_diff + other_diff with NO added separator; returns ("", nil) — NOT an error — when the result is empty.

The exclusion pathspec uses the EXACT contract tokens verbatim (`:!*.lock`, `:!package-lock.json`, `:!pnpm-lock.yaml`, `:!yarn.lock`, `:!*.snap`, `:!*.map`, `:!vendor/*`, `:!.md`, `:!.markdown`) — the literal no-star `:!.md`/`:!.markdown` are preserved faithfully (NOT changed to `:!*.md`), accepting that markdown intentionally also appears in other_diff.

internal/git/diff_test.go is white-box `package git`, stdlib `testing` ONLY, composes the shipped S2 harness (newTempRepo/writeFileStage), drives the REAL git binary (git 2.54.0), and PASSES all 5 MOCKING scenarios: (1) a .md diff per-file line-capped (head -n); (2) a large non-md file total-byte-capped (head -c); (3) .lock/.snap/.map/vendor/*/package-lock.json excluded; (4) md+other both present and concatenated md-first; (5) nothing staged -> ("", nil).
